fields.name: subjectCode
fields.description:
Unique allocate plus subject identifier used in both RSS and SMS to reference a unit of study.

.Example:
- `"ACCT101_CL_S1_ON-CAMPUS"`

fields.name: activityGroupCode
fields.description:
Code used to separate activities into logical groups.

.Example:
-`LEC` for lectures,
- `TUT` for tutorials, or
- full names like `Workshop`.

fields.name: numFlaggedTimeslots
fields.description:
The number of timeslots in this activity group that have been flagged.

Flagging may indicate issues such as clashes, over-enrolments,
or capacity problems.

fields.name: status
fields.description:
Current status of this activity group.

Typically reflects whether the group is OFF, ALLOCATION_ADJUSTMENT, etc.
See `GroupStatus` enum for possible values.

fields.name: autoSingle
fields.description:
Indicates whether this group is included in Auto Single sorting.

Auto Single is an automated process that allocates students into activities.

fields.name: minPrefs
fields.description:
The minimum number of preferences a student must provide
for this activity group (used in preference sorts).

Example: 3 (student must rank at least 3 tutorials).

fields.name: isAllowJustification
fields.description:
Flag indicating whether students can provide a justification
for their allocation preferences.

Example: true = justification allowed.

fields.name: isAllowWaitList
fields.description:
Flag indicating whether students are allowed to join a waitlist
when an activity in this group is full.

Example: true = waitlist enabled.

fields.name: message
fields.description:
A message associated with this activity group.

Typically used to display important information or restrictions
to students during the allocation period.

fields.name: isShowAvailability
fields.description:
Flag indicating whether availability (seats remaining)
should be shown to students for this activity group.

fields.name: activities
fields.description:
The list of activities within this activity group.

.Arguments:
- activityCode (optional): Restrict results to a specific activity instance (e.g., "TUT01").

fields.name: allocated
fields.description:
List of current allocations in this activity group.

.Arguments:
- activityCode (optional): Restrict allocations to a specific activity.

fields.name: unallocated
fields.description:
List of students who are enrolled in this subject but have **not yet been allocated**
to any activity within this group.

fields.name: allocatedStudentCodeList
fields.description:
A distinct list of student codes allocated to this activity group.

Example: ["s1234567", "s2345678"]

fields.name: waitlist
fields.description:
Students who are on the waitlist for changes to their timetable
within this activity group.

fields.name: source
fields.description:
Indicates where the allocation record originated from.

.Generally it is broken into three categories:
- Student initiated changes (e.g. via student within AllocatePLUS)
- Staff initiated changes (e.g. via the UI, manual allocations, admin changes within AllocatePLUS).
- System initiated changes (e.g. automatic allocation processes, sorts,waitlist processing, realtime).
- External system changes (e.g. via API import, Batch Import, Activity Deletion).
- Manual changes (e.g. via JDR Software making changes directly in the database on customer request).

.Examples:
- `SYSTEM`: _Forced Sort (`SORT-CODE`)_
-`STUDENT`, _Allocation Adjustment by student (`STUDENT_CODE`)_
-`STAFF`: _Staff deletion (Justification)_
-`MANUAL`: _ASD-CODE Reason for manual allocation change_,
-`"IMPORT"`.

**Example:** `"2025-02-01T10:15:30"`

enumValues.description:
Preference Entry (by Activity).

- Students can submit preferences for specific **activity codes** (e.g., TUT01, TUT02).
- Used when preference sorting is based on choosing particular activities.

Example: Students rank tutorials "TUT01", "TUT02", "TUT03".

enumValues.name: PREFERENCE_ENTRY_BY_START_TIME
enumValues.description:
Preference Entry (by Start Time).

- Students can submit preferences based on **start times** instead of specific activity codes.
- Used when activities are interchangeable and only the timeslot matters.

Example: Students rank "Monday 10am", "Tuesday 2pm", "Friday 9am".

enumValues.name: ALLOCATION_ADJUSTMENT
enumValues.description:
Allocation Adjustment

-The current status is ON
- Students can directly allocate themselves into activities, or adjust existing allocations.
- Typically used after preferences have been processed, to allow fine-tuning.

Example: A student moves from TUT01 to TUT02 if a seat is available.

enumValues.name: READ_ONLY
enumValues.description:
Read-Only.

- The current status is ON
- Students can view their allocations but **cannot make changes**.
- Commonly used when allocation adjustments have closed but visibility remains.

Example: A week before semester starts, allocations are locked as READ_ONLY.

enumValues.name: UNAVAILABLE
enumValues.description:
Unavailable.

- Prevents student access to the module entirely.
- Often used during system maintenance or between allocation periods.

Example: During semester break, modules may be set to UNAVAILABLE.

enumValues.name: NULL
enumValues.description:
Null / Unknown status.

- Indicates an unexpected or invalid state.
- Prevents student access.
- This mode should not normally exist in production.

Example: A misconfiguration returns NULL as the group status.

fields.name: saveAllocateEnrolment
fields.description:
Save a list of **current** student enrolments theyby replacing any existing enrolments for each student provided.
This is a *full refresh* operation driven by the Student Management System (SMS): the data provided here is treated as authoritative _at the time of the call_.

.Operations
* The input is a list of `SaveStudentEnrolmentInput` objects.
* Each `SaveStudentEnrolmentInput` identifies a single student by `studentCode` 	and includes their `currentEnrolments`.
* For that student, the system performs a **replace-all**:
** Any existing enrolments that are not listed in the `currentEnrolments` data are **removed**	 (student is de-enrolled and deallocated).
** Each item in `currentEnrolments` is added if new, ignored/replaced if	already present).
** If `currentEnrolments` is an empty list, **all enrolments for that `studentCode` is removed**. This will completely deallocate the student from all subjects	and is **irreversible**.
* The process scope is **per student**. One student failing validation	does not prevent others from being processed.

.Example Input: 	To set the current enrolments for a student to units A and C, removing any others.
```graphql
currentEnrolments: [
 { unitCode: "A", unitTeachingPeriod: "S1", unitYear: 2025, unitCampus: "NCL", unitClass: "01", unitMode: "ONCAMPUS" },
 { unitCode: "C", unitTeachingPeriod: "S1", unitYear: 2025, unitCampus: "NCL", unitClass: "01", unitMode: "ONCAMPUS" }
]
```

.This mutation utilises a **"replace-all-enrolments-with-this-exact-set"** approach:
* For each student, the set of enrolments	provided in `currentEnrolmentDetail` is treated as the *authoritative* and complete set of current enrolments.
* Existing enrolments in Allocate PLUS that are **not** present in the incoming set will be	**removed** (treated as a de-enrollment event).
* Enrolments present in the incoming set will be **upserted** (inserted if new, updated if	already present).
* The end result is that, for each student, Allocate PLUS exactly matches the input set.

NOTE: This flow is intentionally similar to the Allocate PLUS real-time enrolment update that occurs at login, 	but is **pushed** from the SMS on its own schedule rather than **pulled** by Allocate PLUS at login.

WARNING:  **Destructive and Irreversible**:  This call removes any enrolments not included in the provided set(s). Use with care.

add.version: 25.0.3.0

.Arguments:
- `currentEnrolmentDetail: [<<SaveStudentEnrolmentInput>>]` 	A list (maximum 10 items per request) of enrolment descriptors/data. 	Each entry includes a studentCode and their complete list of current enrolments.
- _RETURNS_ `<<SaveResponse>>` One response per processed input item (or grouped scope). 	Each `SaveResponse` indicates whether an upsert, delete, or no-op occurred and includes 	any server-assigned identifiers, version/etag (if supported), and validation errors.

Asynchronous execution.

The mutation is accepted and processed in the background. The client
may receive a job, batch or row ID or status handle instead of the final result.

enumValues.name: CREATE
enumValues.description: Create a new row, object, type or entity.

enumValues.name: UPDATE
enumValues.description: Update an existing row, object, type or entity.

enumValues.name: REJECT
enumValues.description: Reject the row, object, type or entity; it will not be processed further.

enumValues.name: DELETE
enumValues.description: Deletes an existing record.

enumValues.name: ACCEPT
enumValues.description: Accept the row, object, type or entity; it will proceed to further processing.

enumValues.name: UNCHANGED
enumValues.description: No changes applied to the row, object, type or entity.

enumValues.name: CREATED
enumValues.description: A new item was created.

enumValues.name: UPDATED
enumValues.description: An existing item was changed, modified or updated.

enumValues.name: DELETED
enumValues.description: An item was removed or flagged as deleted.

enumValues.name: ASC
enumValues.description: Ascending sort order (A–Z, 0–9, earliest to latest).

enumValues.name: DESC
enumValues.description: Descending sort order (Z–A, 9–0, latest to earliest).

**INTERNAL**: Return the configured start date as a `LocalDate` which evaluates to `YYYY-MM-DD`.

**PERMISSION**: JDR-ONLY

fields.name: weekPatternDates
fields.description:
**INTERNAL:** This is intended for testing the underlying data functions.

**PERMISSION**: JDR-ONLY

Week Pattern Dates for given week pattern.

- `weekPattern` : A required field.  in the format `0001001011` where the `0` represents a non-event week, and the `1` represents an event week.
- `dayOfWeek` : An optional field, of the format `DayOfWeek` where it will assume `TUESDAY` if not included in the call.
- `startTime` : An optional field, of the format `HH:MM` where it will assume `00:00` if not included in the call.
- `weekPatternStartDate` : A optional date for the start of the given week pattern, or it will assume the system week pattern start date. This is in the format `YYYY/MM/DD`
- _RETURNS_: An array of `string` dates, in the format `YYYY-MM-DD`, no time zone information.

fields.name: filterSubjects
fields.description:
Filter Subjects by partial matches.

**INTERNAL:**:

- `subjectCode` : One or more subject codes utilised to filter the response.
- `semester` : One or more semester codes utilised to filter the response.
- `campus` : One or more campus codes utilised to filter the response.
- `isVisibleOnTimetable` : Boolean, the default is to exclude subjects or sections in off mode, this can be overridden.
- `department` : A string representing the department code.
- `faculty` : A string representing the faculty code.
- `manager` : A string representing the manager code.
- `pageNumber` : A number starting at 0, representing what page your are on for your pagination.
- `pageSize` : A number starting at 1, representing how many results to return per page.
- _RETURNS_: a list of type `Subject`, and when no pagination is supplied, will return `10` results.

.eg:
  Multiple Subjects - filterSubjects (subjectCode :`["FINA2222_SEM-1_MEL", "FINA2222_SEM-1_MEL"]`)
  Single Subjects   - filterSubjects (subjectCode :"FINA2222_SEM-1_MEL")

- `pageSize`: Int - Specifies the number of pages to be returned in the pagination. A minimum 1, up to a maximum of 100, defaults to 10 when not specified.

in this subject (via Allocate Plus).

fields.description:
A distinct list of student codes that are officially enrolled
in this subject (via SMS).

Example: ["s1234567", "s3456789"]

fields.name: allocated
fields.description:
List of allocations for this SMS subject.
These allocations may translate to the linked Allocate Plus subject code.

fields.name: enrolled
fields.description:
List of enrolments for this SMS subject.
Only students enrolled in this subject (via SMS) are returned.

fields.name: translatedSubjectCode
fields.description:
The corresponding subject code that acts as the inverse mapping
to `subjectCode`.

In practice, this may be either the “translated” subject code
or the Allocate Plus enrolled subject code.

fields.name: allocationSubject
fields.description: The subject entity linked to the allocation subject type.

fields.name: enrolledSubject
fields.description: The subject entity linked to the enrolled (translated) subject type.

fields.name: unallocatedStudentCodeList
fields.description:
A list of student codes that are enrolled in this subject (via SMS)
but have **no allocations at all** in Allocate Plus.

Useful for detecting students who still need activity assignments.

Example: ["s1234567", "s3456789"]

organised into a `ClassList` of entries.

inputFields.name: currentEnrolments
inputFields.description:
The full list of enrolments to be saved for this student (matching `studentCode`).

Behavior:
* The provided list **replaces** any existing enrolments.
* Any enrolments not included in this list will be **removed**.

WARNING: Passing an empty list will remove **all** existing enrolments.

WARNING: This operation is irreversible and will deallocate all student allocations.

fields.description:
Internal unique identifier for the waitlist entry.
Used by the system to track the request.

fields.name: studentCode
fields.description:
The identifier of the student in the Student Management System (SMS).

Typically this is a student ID, number, or code.
Example: `"s1234567"`

fields.name: subjectCode
fields.description:
Unique allocate plus subject identifier used in both RSS and SMS to reference a unit of study.

.Example:
- `"ACCT101_CL_S1_ON-CAMPUS"`

fields.name: activityGroupCode
fields.description:
Code used to separate activities into logical groups.

.Example:
-`LEC` for lectures,
- `TUT` for tutorials, or
- full names like `Workshop`.

fields.name: sourceActivityCode
fields.description:
The **source activity code** (the activity the student is currently in).

Example: "TUT01"

fields.name: destinationActivityCode
fields.description:
The **destination activity code** (the activity the student wants to move into).

Example: "TUT02"

fields.name: status
fields.description:
The current status of this waitlist entry.

Examples:
- "COMPLETED" or "C"   = Request was successfully fulfilled (student moved).
- "DELETED" or "D"    = Request was deleted by the student.
- "INVALIDATED" or "I" = Request could not be completed due to external factors (e.g., student withdrew).
- "PENDING" or "P"     = Request is still to be processed.

fields.name: message
fields.description:
Additional human-readable message about the current status.

Examples:
- "Deleted: Deleted By Student"
- "Invalidated: New Allocation Adjustment"
- Request Fulfilled

fields.name: dateRequested
fields.description:
Date and time when the waitlist request was first created.
Format: `yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss`

fields.name: dateAttempted
fields.description:
Date and time when the system last attempted to process this waitlist entry.
Format: `yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss`

fields.name: dateCompleted
fields.description:
Date and time when the waitlist request was completed (successful or failed).
May be `null` if the request is still pending.

fields.name: swapAttempts
fields.description:
A history of attempts made by the system to complete this waitlist request.

Each `SwapAttempt` records details of the processing attempt.

fields.description: The weekpattern date in the format YYYY-MM-DD

fields.name: time
fields.description: A time string in the format of 09:30 13:00 etc

fields.description: The Allocate+ day of week representation.
